content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807                    SACRAMENTO 95804
107 SOUTH BROADWAY            LOS ANGELES, 90012
                              SUITE 7007

July 8, 1981

A-81-165

Ms. Mary Lou Rushing
Republican Central Committee
777 South Main, Suite 200
Orange, California 92668

Dear Ms. Rushing:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to you during our
telephone conversation this date.

     The facts as I understand them to be are as follows:
A member of the County Republican Central Committee wrote a
personal check to pay for printing goods received by this person
for the County Central Committee.  The member was reimbursed
by another person who was, in turn, reimbursed by the state
organization of the Republican party.

     The question you asked was since the County Central
Committee received an in-kind contribution from the State
Republican Party organization and will report the contribution
as coming from the state organization, is it necessary to
report the two individuals that wrote personal checks for the
transaction as intermediaries?

     I advised that both individuals that wrote their own
personal checks and were ultimately reimbursed by the state
organization of the Republican party must be reported as
"intermediaries" pursuant to Government Code Section 84302.

     I hope this information has been helpful to you.  Please
let me know if I can be of any further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Helen P. Arriola
                                        Political Reform
                                        Consultant

HPA:jg


SACRAMENTO (916) 222-5660        •        LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question: Since the County Central Committee received an in-kind contribution from the State Republican Party organization and will report the contribution as coming from the state organization, is it necessary to report the two individuals that wrote personal checks for the transaction as intermediaries?
sections.conclusion: Both individuals that wrote their own personal checks and were ultimately reimbursed by the state organization of the Republican party must be reported as "intermediaries" pursuant to Government Code Section 84302.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether individuals who wrote personal checks for printing goods received by a County Central Committee and were subsequently reimbursed by the State Republican Party must be reported as intermediaries in campaign contribution disclosures.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that both individuals who wrote personal checks and were reimbursed by the State Republican Party organization must be reported as intermediaries under Government Code Section 84302.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal section headers present; question and conclusion reconstructed from letter body. Telephone advice confirmed in writing. Clear intermediary reporting requirement identified.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Since the County Central Committee received an in-kind contribution from the State Republican Party organization and will report the contribution as coming from the state organization, is it necessary to report the two individuals that wrote personal checks for the transaction as intermediaries?

CONCLUSION: Both individuals that wrote their own personal checks and were ultimately reimbursed by the state organization of the Republican party must be reported as "intermediaries" pursuant to Government Code Section 84302.

The facts as I understand them to be are as follows: A member of the County Republican Central Committee wrote a personal check to pay for printing goods received by this person for the County Central Committee. The member was reimbursed by another person who was, in turn, reimbursed by the state organization of the Republican party. The question you asked was since the County Central Committee received an in-kind contribution from the State Republican Party organization and will report the contribution as coming from the state organization, is it necessary to report the two individuals that wrote personal checks for the transaction as intermediaries? I advised that both individuals that wrote their own personal checks and were ultimately reimbursed by the state organization of the Republican party must be reported as "intermediaries" pursuant to Government Code Section 84302. I hope this information has been helpful to you. Please let me know if I can be of any further assistance. Sincerely, Helen P. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that individuals who personally paid for in-kind contributions (printing goods) to a County Central Committee that were later reimbursed by the State Republican Party must be disclosed as intermediaries in campaign finance reports.
